summary:
The primary location for chief cells within gastric glands includes the fundus and body sections. These cells secrete pepsinogen into the stomach lumen as part of digestion processes but do not directly influence blood glucose levels or hormone secretion related questions asked here.
To refine this summary while adhering strictly to your instructions:

Chief cells reside primarily in the fundus and body portions of gastric glands. They produce pepsinogen essential for protein breakdown during digestion.
